other_observers: I observed the bird with Scott Rashid (original finder), John Vanderpoel, and Peter Gent; Found by Scott Rashid 2 June
specific_location: West shore of Middle Steele Lake, West of Lagerman Reservoir 40deg 8min 14.97sec N 105deg 12min 53.23sec W
description: Small, mousy bird with habitually cocked tail in slender, decurved bill, clearly a wren. Maxilla mostly gray, mandible yellow. Dark eye with weak supercillium. Crown streaked with alternating light and dark brown stripes. Throat & upper breast pale gray. Flanks buffy reddish brown, feet & legs dull pink. Wings very checkered looking, including checkered scapulars. Back streaked with white-edged dark brown stripes. Tail short, barred, often cocked upright.Behaviors: The bird responded to pre-recorded playback, emerging from tall wet grass into low, brushy Russian Olives where it called and sang.Call: Bird sang and called in response to recorded playback. Calls and song the bird gave matched well with pre-recorded tracks.Plumage: adult
